name: sdd
description: Spec-Driven Development pipeline for Claude Code: 14 atomic skills that carry a feature from idea → spec → design → data model → API → tasks → tests → implementation → review → ship, plus a TDD implementation engine with agent-team and dynamic-workflow modes. Start with specify — it interviews you and writes the spec; then walk the backbone in order; classify-size / glossary / decide-adr are utilities you call anytime. Skills are stack-agnostic and Socratic: classify-size (XS/S/M/L/XL gate), specify (lightweight interview that creates the spec + acceptance criteria, Socratic per-AC + clean-context critic), clarify (devil's-advocate ambiguity sweep before planning), design (matches the feature to the existing architecture via a brownfield Explore scan, then Arc42 SAD + C4 L1/L2 + inline ADRs with a blast-radius gate), sequences (Mermaid sequence diagrams with generic participants), data-model (data-model.md + paired forward/rollback migrations + drift check), api (OpenAPI 3.1 derived from data-model + sequences + spec), tasks (breakdown emitting a tracker + tasks.json DAG contract), plan-tests (AC-to-test mapping), implement (TDD red→green→refactor engine that reads tasks.json, builds a dependency DAG, runs sequential / agent-team / dynamic-workflow modes behind feature flags, with a per-task unit+integration+lint+vet gate), review (independent clean-context code review of the whole change against spec/AC + quality, can bounce back to implement), ship (verifies the feature actually runs, writes the changelog, opens the PR — never auto-merges), decide-adr (post-hoc ADRs), glossary (lazy CONTEXT.md). Shared Socratic / critic / size-matrix / ask-style logic lives once in skills/_shared and is referenced, not duplicated. Three subagents ship with the engine: sdd-test-author (writes failing tests), sdd-implementer (makes them pass), sdd-reviewer (read-only spec/AC + quality review). GATE skills hard-refuse when a prerequisite artifact is missing. Each SKILL.md is the source of truth; heavy detail lives in references/, output templates in templates/.
